title: UPSC Civil Services Prelims 2026 — General Studies Paper I (Official)

questions.subject: History
questions.text:
Consider the following assertion:
In the Pleistocene period either the Yamuna once flowed into the Indus, or the Sutlej flowed into the Yamuna and one major tributary of either had shifted from the Ganga to the Indus or vice versa.
Which of the following is/are the basis of the above assertion?
1. The Nadi-Sukta of the Rigveda
2. The explorations of the Sutlej and the Yamuna by Robert Bruce Foote
3. The presence of the same species of dolphins in both the Indus and the Ganga river systems
Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.explanation: The Nadi-Sukta records Vedic-period river knowledge, not Pleistocene shifts. Robert Bruce Foote is tied to prehistoric stone tools, not Sutlej–Yamuna drainage. The same freshwater dolphin species in both the Indus and Ganga systems is the valid biogeographic basis. Only statement 3.
questions.elimination_path: Mark each statement true/false first, then eliminate every option that contains a statement you can rule out.

questions.subject: History
questions.text: What does an empty seat represent in early Buddhist iconography?
questions.explanation: Early Buddhist art was aniconic. The empty seat/throne stood for the Buddha's meditation/enlightenment. Wheel = First Sermon, Stupa = Mahaparinibbana, Horse = Mahabhinishkramana.
questions.elimination_path: Eliminate the clearly wrong options first, then choose between what remains.

questions.subject: History
questions.text:
Which of the following pairs of ancient and modern names of rivers is/are correctly matched?
1. Vitasta : Chenab
2. Asikni : Jhelum
3. Parushni : Ravi
4. Yavyavati : Beas
Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.explanation: Vitasta = Jhelum, Asikni = Chenab (so 1 and 2 are swapped), Parushni = Ravi (correct), Beas = Vipasha (so 4 wrong). Only pair 3.
questions.elimination_path: Mark each statement true/false first, then eliminate every option that contains a statement you can rule out.

questions.subject: History
questions.text:
Which of the following statements on the Amaravati Stupa and its relief sculpture is/are correct?
1. It was located in the lower Krishna valley.
2. In India, it was next only to the Sanchi Stupa in size.
3. The Amaravati school of sculpture made a lasting impact on the later South Indian sculpture, and its products were carried to Sri Lanka and South-east Asia.
Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.explanation: Amaravati was in the lower Krishna valley (1 correct) and influenced later South Indian, Sri Lankan and SE-Asian sculpture (3 correct). Statement 2 is wrong — when intact it was larger than Sanchi.
questions.elimination_path: Mark each statement true/false first, then eliminate every option that contains a statement you can rule out.

questions.subject: History
questions.text:
Which of the following pairs of the king and his dynasty in early historical Tamilakam is/are NOT correctly matched?
1. Senguttuvan : Chera
2. Udiyanjeral : Chola
3. Nedunjeliyan : Pandya
Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.explanation: Senguttuvan = Chera and Nedunjeliyan = Pandya are correct. Udiyanjeral was a Chera, not a Chola — so only pair 2 is NOT correctly matched.
questions.elimination_path: Mark each statement true/false first, then eliminate every option that contains a statement you can rule out.

questions.subject: History
questions.text:
Which of the following factors contributed to the formation of the Forward Bloc by Subhas Chandra Bose in 1939?
1. Bose failed to win the confidence of Mahatma Gandhi.
2. The Congress Left was disunited and failed to support Bose.
3. The Communists did not support Bose in his endeavours.
4. The supporters of M.N. Roy and socialist leaders like Jayaprakash Narayan preferred Congress unity to supporting Bose.
Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.explanation: Loss of Gandhi's confidence, a fragmented Congress Left, and the Roy/socialist preference for Congress unity all pushed Bose out (1, 2, 4). The Communist stance (3) is not treated as a direct factor.
questions.elimination_path: Mark each statement true/false first, then eliminate every option that contains a statement you can rule out.

questions.subject: History
questions.text:
Consider the following statements regarding the British policy in Awadh immediately after its annexation in 1856:
1. The taluqdars were dispossessed of their estates but allowed to retain their arms and forts.
2. A Summary Revenue Settlement was made in 1856 assuming that the taluqdars were outsiders.
3. The British believed in taking revenue directly from the peasants by removing the taluqdars.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: The British made a Summary Settlement bypassing taluqdars and sought revenue directly from peasants (2, 3 correct). Statement 1 is wrong — taluqdars were not allowed to keep arms and forts.
questions.elimination_path: Mark each statement true/false first, then eliminate every option that contains a statement you can rule out.

questions.subject: History
questions.text:
Consider the following assertion:
The genesis of political alliances based on community lay in the very nature of the Montague-Chelmsford Reforms, 1919.
Which of the following statements support/supports the above assertion?
1. Reforms retained and extended the principle of separate electorates.
2. Separate electorates were supposed to counter Indian nationalism, which was growing stronger.
3. Deprived classes rallied around the favours inherent in separate electorates.
Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.explanation: The reforms retained and expanded separate electorates, used them to counter rising nationalism, and various groups rallied around their benefits. All three support the assertion.
questions.elimination_path: Mark each statement true/false first, then eliminate every option that contains a statement you can rule out.

questions.subject: History
questions.text: Pandit Mallikarjun Mansur, the famous classical singer from Karnataka, represented the:
questions.explanation: Pandit Mallikarjun Mansur was a renowned Hindustani vocalist of the Jaipur-Atrauli Gharana.
questions.elimination_path: Eliminate the clearly wrong options first, then choose between what remains.

questions.subject: History
questions.text: In which one among the following texts does the term kshetra-patni ("mistress of the field") originate?
questions.explanation: The term kshetra-patni ("mistress of the field") is associated with the Atharvaveda, which carries references linked to fertility, agriculture and domestic rituals.
questions.elimination_path: Eliminate the clearly wrong options first, then choose between what remains.

questions.subject: History
questions.text: Which one of the following Carnatic music ragas is similar to Raga Bilawal in Hindustani music?
questions.explanation: The Carnatic raga Dheera Shankarabharanam corresponds broadly to the Hindustani Bilawal — both are major-scale type ragas.
questions.elimination_path: Eliminate the clearly wrong options first, then choose between what remains.

questions.subject: History
questions.text: The artificially fixed rupee-sterling exchange rate prescribed by the Hilton-Young Commission, 1926, was adopted by the British Government for which one of the following reasons?

options.A: Aiding the flow of remittances from India and maintaining India's creditworthiness
options.C: Encouraging export of cotton produce from India
options.D: Preventing depreciation of the Rupee in terms of gold

questions.explanation: The fixed rate served colonial interests by aiding remittances ("home charges") from India and maintaining India's sterling creditworthiness.
questions.elimination_path: Eliminate the clearly wrong options first, then choose between what remains.

questions.subject: History
questions.text:
Consider the following statements:
I. Pali texts contain the first definite references to coins, e.g., kahapana, nikkha, kamsa, and kakanika.
II. The literary evidence from Pali texts is corroborated by archaeological evidence of punch-marked coins from many sites, most of them made of silver.
The above statements have been associated with which of the following?
1. Emergence of urban life
2. Transition to money economy
Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.explanation: Pali references to coins corroborated by punch-marked coin finds indicate both the rise of urban (second urbanisation) life and the transition to a money economy.
questions.elimination_path: Mark each statement true/false first, then eliminate every option that contains a statement you can rule out.

questions.subject: History
questions.text:
Which of the following temples has/have a Nagara-style shikhara?
1. Malegitti Shivalaya, Badami
2. Huchimalligudi Temple, Aihole
3. Dashavatara Temple, Deogarh
4. Virupaksha Temple, Pattadakal
Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.explanation: The Huchimalligudi Temple (Aihole) and Dashavatara Temple (Deogarh) are associated with Nagara-style shikhara features; Malegitti Shivalaya and Virupaksha are not counted here.
questions.elimination_path: Mark each statement true/false first, then eliminate every option that contains a statement you can rule out.

questions.subject: History
questions.text: Among the four main forms of existence of life recognized in Jainism, which one of the following is NOT included?
questions.explanation: Jainism's four forms of existence are Deva, Manushya, Tiryancha and Naraka. Yaksha is not one of them.
questions.elimination_path: Eliminate the clearly wrong options first, then choose between what remains.

questions.subject: History
questions.text: The Hallisalasya painting in the Bagh Caves represents:

options.C: The depiction of Shiva and Parvati on Kailasha

questions.explanation: The Hallisalasya painting in the Bagh Caves depicts a joyous folk dance scene.
questions.elimination_path: Eliminate the clearly wrong options first, then choose between what remains.

questions.subject: History
questions.text:
Consider the following statements relating to the use of the place-value system in India:
1. The earliest epigraphic use of the place-value system in India is found in the Mankani plates from Gujarat, AD 595–596.
2. In the ninth century, place-values become general in inscriptions all over India.
3. The place-values have been found in Sanskrit inscriptions in South-east Asia as early as the seventh century.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanation: All three are correct — earliest epigraphic use in the Mankani plates, general use by the ninth century, and seventh-century occurrence in SE-Asian Sanskrit inscriptions.
questions.elimination_path: Mark each statement true/false first, then eliminate every option that contains a statement you can rule out.

questions.subject: History
questions.text:
Consider the following statements about the archaeological findings in Harappan towns:
I. There is wide occurrence of spindle-whorls in the houses but absence of spinning wheels.
II. Weights and measurement scales, complete with graduations, have been discovered.
III. There are houses built in large part with baked bricks, around relatively spacious courtyards, with their own wells, bathing platforms, and large rooms.
Which of the following inferences can be drawn from the above statements?
1. Statement I suggests that spinning was a laborious activity done at home.
2. Statement II suggests the extent of the scientific knowledge that the Harappans possessed.
3. Statement III suggests the emergence of a common property system.
Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.explanation: Spindle-whorls without wheels imply laborious home spinning (1), graduated scales imply measurement knowledge (2). Large private houses with their own wells do NOT suggest common property (3 wrong).
questions.elimination_path: Mark each statement true/false first, then eliminate every option that contains a statement you can rule out.

questions.subject: History
questions.text: Which one of the following statements about the Eka Movement and Bardoli Satyagraha is correct?

options.A: The Eka Movement was throughout supported and organized by the Congress while Bardoli Satyagraha was initially independent of Congress influence and was only in the last stages supported by the Congress.
options.B: The Eka Movement was provided leadership by the taluqdars of Awadh, whereas the Bardoli Satyagraha was a movement of the landless labourers.
options.C: The Bardoli Satyagraha was a campaign against the enhancement of land revenue, while the Eka Movement was a protest against excessive extraction of rents.
options.D: The Eka Movement was located in the Varanasi and Mirzapur districts of present-day U.P., while the Bardoli Satyagraha took place in Saurashtra.

questions.explanation: Bardoli Satyagraha opposed a land-revenue enhancement; the Eka Movement (Awadh) protested excessive rent extraction. Option (c) gives the correct contrast.
questions.elimination_path: Eliminate the clearly wrong options first, then choose between what remains.

questions.subject: History
questions.text:
Consider the following statements about the Rigvedic period:
I. Irrigation from wells allowed agriculture to expand away from flood plains and strips on river margins into the present Punjab and Haryana plains having underground water levels reasonably close to the surface.
II. Draught-animal power was employed to draw up water out of the wells.
Which of the following information support/supports the above statements?
1. There is evidence in the Rigveda of the use of ashma chakra (stone pulley wheel) and ahava (strapped wooden pails) to draw up water.
2. Mention has been made in the Rigveda of the use of implements like parashu/kulisha (axe) and datra/sreni (sickle).
3. There is a history of the use of ox, even before the Rigveda, for ploughing the land and pulling the carts.
Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.explanation: Ashma chakra and ahava support well-drawing (1); the ox's use as draught power supports drawing water by animal power (3). Axe/sickle (2) show farming but not the specific well-irrigation claim.
questions.elimination_path: Mark each statement true/false first, then eliminate every option that contains a statement you can rule out.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.text: Tungurahua Volcano, which was declared a Global Geopark by UNESCO in 2025, is situated in which one among the following countries?
questions.explanation: Tungurahua Volcano is located in Ecuador.
questions.elimination_path: Eliminate the clearly wrong options first, then choose between what remains.

questions.subject: Environment
questions.text:
With reference to Madhav National Park, which of the following statements is/are correct?
1. It was declared a Tiger Reserve in India in 2025.
2. Sakhya Sagar, which is designated as a Ramsar Site, is situated within this National Park.
3. Its area is shared between Madhya Pradesh and Rajasthan.
Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.explanation: Madhav NP was declared a Tiger Reserve and the Ramsar site Sakhya Sagar lies within it (1, 2). It is wholly in Madhya Pradesh, not shared with Rajasthan (3 wrong).
questions.elimination_path: Mark each statement true/false first, then eliminate every option that contains a statement you can rule out.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.text:
With reference to the climate of Andaman and Nicobar Islands, which of the following statements is/are correct?
1. The climate can be defined as a humid, tropical coastal climate.
2. It receives rainfall from both South-west monsoon and North-east monsoon.
3. Maximum precipitation is between December and May.
Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.explanation: The islands have a humid tropical coastal climate and receive rainfall from both monsoons (1, 2). Maximum precipitation is NOT between December and May (3 wrong).
questions.elimination_path: Mark each statement true/false first, then eliminate every option that contains a statement you can rule out.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.text:
Which of the following geographical features or phenomena is/are associated with the Peninsular Block of India?
1. Submergence of parts of the western coast due to tectonic activity
2. Presence of residual mountain ranges such as the Veliconda hills and Mahendragiri hills
3. Deep, V-shaped river valleys formed by fast-flowing rivers
Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.explanation: The Peninsular Block shows western-coast submergence and residual ranges like Veliconda and Mahendragiri (1, 2). Deep V-shaped valleys belong to youthful Himalayan rivers (3 wrong).
questions.elimination_path: Mark each statement true/false first, then eliminate every option that contains a statement you can rule out.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.text:
Consider the following statements with reference to the Sagarmala Programme of the Government of India:
I. The Sagarmala Programme seeks to achieve port-led economic growth through cost-effective and sustainable coastal infrastructure.
II. The success of the Sagarmala Programme is reflected in significant growth in coastal and inland waterway shipping, along with improved global port rankings.
III. Sagarmala 2.0 aims to position India as a global maritime innovation hub aligned with Atmanirbhar Bharat and Viksit Bharat 2047 visions.
Which of the following relationships among the above statements is/are correct?
1. Statement II validates the effectiveness of the strategies envisioned in statement I.
2. Statement III extends the objectives of statement I by embedding them into a future-oriented innovation framework.
3. Statement I contradicts statement III by focusing only on traditional infrastructure instead of modern innovation.
Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.explanation: II validates I and III extends I (1, 2 correct). I does not contradict III (3 wrong) — both are part of the same evolving vision.
questions.elimination_path: Mark each statement true/false first, then eliminate every option that contains a statement you can rule out.

questions.subject: Environment
questions.text:
Consider the following statements about Rhynchostylis retusa (Foxtail orchid):
1. It is an epiphytic orchid.
2. The species is endemic to North-east India.
3. It is the State flower of Arunachal Pradesh and Assam.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: The Foxtail orchid is epiphytic and is the state flower of Assam and Arunachal Pradesh (1, 3). It is not endemic only to NE India (2 wrong).
questions.elimination_path: Mark each statement true/false first, then eliminate every option that contains a statement you can rule out.

questions.subject: History
questions.text:
Which one of the following statements with regard to the Moidams, built by the Tai-Ahom kingdom and inscribed as a World Heritage Site by UNESCO, is/are correct?
1. They acted as army fortresses.
2. They were recreation centres of the Royals and Nobles.
3. They were burial grounds of the Royals and Nobles.
4. They were battle drill centres of the Royals and Nobles.
Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.explanation: Moidams are royal burial mounds of the Tai-Ahom kingdom — only statement 3 is correct.
questions.elimination_path: Mark each statement true/false first, then eliminate every option that contains a statement you can rule out.

questions.subject: Environment
questions.text:
At the United Nations Ocean Conference (UNOC) held in June 2025 in France, the FAO demonstrated its leading voice on marine and ocean issues, especially on sustainable fisheries and aquaculture for "Blue Transformation".
Which of the following combinations about the "Four Betters" proposed by FAO for "Blue Transformation" is correct?

options.A: Better production, better nutrition, better environment and better ocean
options.B: Better production, better nutrition, better environment and better life
options.C: Better coral reefs, better nutrition, better environment and better life
options.D: Better estuaries, better nutrition, better environment and better mangrove vegetation

questions.explanation: FAO's "Four Betters" are better production, better nutrition, better environment and better life.
questions.elimination_path: Eliminate the clearly wrong options first, then choose between what remains.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.text:
Which of the following statements with reference to Lake Turkana is/are correct?
1. It is the largest desert lake in the world.
2. The lake is situated in South Sudan along the eastern fringe of the Sahara desert.
3. The lake is listed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site and is also referred to as the "Jade Sea".
Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.explanation: Lake Turkana is the world's largest desert lake and a UNESCO site called the "Jade Sea" (1, 3). It lies in Kenya (extending to Ethiopia), not South Sudan/Sahara (2 wrong).
questions.elimination_path: Mark each statement true/false first, then eliminate every option that contains a statement you can rule out.

questions.subject: Environment
questions.text: Which one of the following is the first Plan Vivo certified Reducing Emissions from Deforestation and Forest Degradation (REDD+) project in India?

options.B: ICFRE-ICIMOD Transboundary REDD+ project in North-Eastern Himalayas
options.D: Sikkim Mamley Kamrang Community REDD+ project

questions.explanation: The Khasi Hills Community REDD+ Project is India's first Plan Vivo certified REDD+ project.
questions.elimination_path: Eliminate the clearly wrong options first, then choose between what remains.

questions.subject: Environment
questions.text:
Consider the following statements with reference to India's response to climate change:
I. India's Long-Term Low Emission Development Strategy (LT-LEDS) is a crucial tool for achieving net-zero emissions by 2070.
II. India's 4th Biennial Update Report (BUR-4) submitted in December 2024 recorded around 8% decrease in greenhouse gas emissions in 2020 over 2019.
III. Climate-resilient development necessarily depends on quick and short-term achievement of emission reduction targets.
Which of the following relationships among the above statements is/are correct?
1. Statement II empirically supports statement I.
2. Statement III contradicts the approach implicit in statement I.
3. Statement I and statement III together establish the premise of long-term sustainability.
Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.explanation: II (lower emissions) empirically supports I, and III (short-term dependence) contradicts I's long-term approach (1, 2 correct). I and III do not together establish long-term sustainability (3 wrong).
questions.elimination_path: Mark each statement true/false first, then eliminate every option that contains a statement you can rule out.

questions.subject: Environment
questions.text:
With respect to the Western Hoolock Gibbons, which of the following statements is/are correct?
1. A sanctuary in North-east India is home to this ape species listed as Endangered in the IUCN Red List.
2. They have specialized brachiation and can easily swing between trees.
3. They possess a strong and heavy build like gorillas, yet are remarkably agile tree climbers.
Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.explanation: Endangered NE-India apes known for brachiation (1, 2). They are slender, not gorilla-like in build (3 wrong).
questions.elimination_path: Mark each statement true/false first, then eliminate every option that contains a statement you can rule out.

questions.subject: Environment
questions.text:
Which of the following best explain(s) the rationale for protecting mangrove ecosystems in the context of climate resilience?
1. Mangroves reduce tidal energy and store freshwater, making them ideal sites for paddy cultivation in saline estuarine belts.
2. Their salt-sensitive roots filter seawater, making mangroves key to converting coastal land into freshwater aquaculture zones.
3. By withstanding tidal surges and offering biomass resources, mangroves function both as natural bio-shields and livelihood bases for rural communities.
Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.explanation: Mangroves are natural bio-shields and livelihood bases (3 correct). They do not convert saline land into freshwater zones or make paddy/aquaculture "ideal" (1, 2 wrong).
questions.elimination_path: Mark each statement true/false first, then eliminate every option that contains a statement you can rule out.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.text:
In what way(s) does the Vizhinjam International Seaport represent a structural shift in India's maritime trade and logistics policy?
1. By functioning exclusively as a domestic cargo hub to reduce reliance on coastal shipping and eliminate the need for foreign collaborations.
2. By focusing primarily on passenger cruise tourism and heritage shipping to increase Kerala's profile as a maritime heritage destination.
3. By leveraging its natural deep draft and strategic location to reduce dependence on foreign trans-shipment ports, enhance revenue retention, and reposition India in regional maritime trade.
Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.explanation: Vizhinjam's deep draft and location reduce dependence on foreign trans-shipment ports (3 correct). It is neither domestic-only nor cruise-tourism focused (1, 2 wrong).
questions.elimination_path: Mark each statement true/false first, then eliminate every option that contains a statement you can rule out.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.text:
Identify the river of the Indian sub-continent on the basis of the following information:
1. It has an antecedent drainage system.
2. It flows through three countries.
3. It originates in the Tibetan Plateau and is an important river for irrigation.
4. It does not form distributaries.
Select the answer from the following:
questions.explanation: The Sutlej has antecedent drainage, originates near the Tibetan Plateau, flows through China/Tibet, India and Pakistan, is vital for irrigation, and forms no distributaries.
questions.elimination_path: Mark each statement true/false first, then eliminate every option that contains a statement you can rule out.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.text:
Which of the following with reference to Indian States is/are NOT correct?
1. Uttar Pradesh shares its boundary with the highest number of other Indian States.
2. Rajasthan shares the longest international border among all Indian States.
3. Sikkim is the only State that shares its boundary with just one other Indian State.
Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.explanation: Statement 1 is correct. Rajasthan does not have the longest international border when all are considered (2 NOT correct), and Sikkim is not the only single-neighbour state — Meghalaya borders only Assam (3 NOT correct).
questions.elimination_path: Mark each statement true/false first, then eliminate every option that contains a statement you can rule out.

questions.subject: Environment
questions.text:
Which of the following statements with regard to the arrival of Amur Falcons at Doyang Lake in Nagaland each year from Mongolia is/are correct?
1. It showcases how sustained local conservation efforts can contribute to the arrival and protection of international migratory birds.
2. It reflects the global success of advanced tracking technologies that guide migratory birds back to their stopover sites.
3. It confirms that Amur Falcons have adapted to permanent residency in India due to favourable habitat changes.
Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.explanation: Their return reflects successful local conservation (1 correct). Tracking technologies monitor but do not "guide" the birds (2 wrong), and the falcons remain migratory, not residents (3 wrong).
questions.elimination_path: Mark each statement true/false first, then eliminate every option that contains a statement you can rule out.

questions.subject: Environment
questions.text:
Which among the following is/are the objective(s) of the Rainfed Area Development (RAD) initiative under the National Mission for Sustainable Agriculture (NMSA)?
1. Encouraging monoculture in rainfed areas
2. Increasing rice cultivation in irrigated regions
3. Enhancing productivity and minimising climatic risks through Integrated Farming Systems (IFS)
Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.explanation: RAD promotes Integrated Farming Systems to raise productivity and cut climate risk in rainfed areas (3). It does not encourage monoculture or irrigated rice expansion (1, 2 wrong).
questions.elimination_path: Mark each statement true/false first, then eliminate every option that contains a statement you can rule out.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.text:
Which of the following is/are the most significant implication(s) of obtaining Oeko-Tex certification for Eri Silk in the global textile industry?
1. It allows Indian exporters to compete in high-end markets that prioritise chemical-free products.
2. It confirms that Eri Silk meets international safety, environmental, and quality standards, enabling its entry into premium eco-conscious markets.
Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.explanation: Oeko-Tex confirms safety/environmental standards and opens premium, eco-conscious and chemical-free markets — both statements are correct.
questions.elimination_path: Mark each statement true/false first, then eliminate every option that contains a statement you can rule out.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.text:
Ships from which of the following countries have to cross the Strait of Hormuz to reach out to the Indian Ocean?
1. Bahrain
2. Syria
3. Qatar
4. Egypt
Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.explanation: Bahrain and Qatar lie inside the Persian Gulf and must cross the Strait of Hormuz (1, 3). Syria and Egypt do not need Hormuz for Indian Ocean access.
questions.elimination_path: Mark each statement true/false first, then eliminate every option that contains a statement you can rule out.

questions.subject: CurrentAffairs
questions.text:
Match List I with List II and select the answer using the code given below the Lists:
List I (INTERPOL Notice): A. Silver Notice  B. Blue Notice  C. Black Notice  D. Green Notice
List II (Description): 1. To seek information on unidentified bodies  2. To collect additional information about a person's identity, location, or activities in relation to a criminal investigation  3. To provide warning about a person's criminal activities, where the person is considered to be a possible threat to public safety  4. To identify and trace criminal assets
questions.explanation: Silver = trace criminal assets (4), Blue = additional information about a person (2), Black = unidentified bodies (1), Green = warning about a person's criminal activities (3). The correct combination is A-4, B-2, C-1, D-3.
questions.elimination_path: Mark each statement true/false first, then eliminate every option that contains a statement you can rule out.

questions.subject: Environment
questions.text:
Which of the following statements in relation to NIRANTAR — a platform of institutions under the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change — is/are correct?
1. Ecosystem Survey and Analysis is a vertical under this platform, the lead institute of which is Botanical Survey of India, Kolkata.
2. Research and Management of Ecosystem Service is a vertical under this platform, the lead institute of which is Central Zoo Authority, New Delhi.
3. Capacity Development Support is a vertical under this platform, the lead institute of which is Indian Institute of Forest Management, Bhopal.
Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.explanation: Ecosystem Survey & Analysis is led by the Botanical Survey of India and Capacity Development Support by IIFM Bhopal (1, 3). The Central Zoo Authority is not the lead for the ecosystem-service vertical (2 wrong).
questions.elimination_path: Mark each statement true/false first, then eliminate every option that contains a statement you can rule out.

questions.subject: CurrentAffairs
questions.text:
The Chancellor of the Federal Republic of Germany visited India in January 2026.
Which of the following is/are NOT correct in terms of outcomes of this visit?
1. Signing of a Memorandum of Understanding between the All India Institute of Ayurveda and the University of Hamburg
2. Signing of a Memorandum of Understanding on Youth Hockey Development between Hockey India and the German Hockey Federation
3. Establishment of a bilateral dialogue mechanism on the Indo-Pacific
4. Opening of an Honorary Consul of Germany in Lucknow
Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.explanation: The question asks which outcomes are NOT correct — the Ayurveda–Hamburg MoU and the Honorary Consul in Lucknow are not correct (1, 4). The hockey MoU and Indo-Pacific dialogue are correct outcomes.
questions.elimination_path: Mark each statement true/false first, then eliminate every option that contains a statement you can rule out.

questions.subject: SciTech
questions.text:
Which of the following statements about DHRUV64 is/are correct?
1. It is the third chip fabricated under the DIR-V Programme with an overall aim to enable the creation of microprocessors for India.
2. It is India's first homegrown 1.0 GHz, 64-bit dual-core microprocessor.
Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.explanation: DHRUV64 is the third chip under the DIR-V Programme and India's first homegrown 1.0 GHz, 64-bit dual-core microprocessor — both correct.
questions.elimination_path: Mark each statement true/false first, then eliminate every option that contains a statement you can rule out.

questions.subject: SciTech
questions.text:
The Bureau of Indian Standard (BIS) recently introduced a national standard to test and assess bomb disposal system.
Which of the following statements with regard to this system is/are correct?
1. The new standard is known as IS 19445 : 2025.
2. It will improve interoperability of equipment across agencies.
3. It was developed by TBRL, DRDO in collaboration with the 30th Central Scientific Research Institute, Russia.
Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.explanation: The standard IS 19445:2025 improves interoperability across agencies (1, 2). The claim of development with the Russian institute is not correct (3 wrong).
questions.elimination_path: Mark each statement true/false first, then eliminate every option that contains a statement you can rule out.

questions.subject: SciTech
questions.text: 'X', born in the UK, was conferred the Nobel Prize in 2025. He was a professor in an American university when this prize was announced. Identify 'X':
questions.explanation: 'X' is John Clarke — UK-born, working at an American university when the 2025 prize was announced.
questions.elimination_path: Eliminate the clearly wrong options first, then choose between what remains.

questions.subject: CurrentAffairs
questions.text:
Which of the following statements with regard to the Grand Slam Tennis Tournaments is/are correct?
1. The tournaments have a shared governance structure establishing the partnership among the four Grand Slam tournaments.
2. They are open for entry to all internationally ranked tennis players above the age of 14.
3. There is a limitation on the number of "Wild Cards" a player may receive to compete in a Grand Slam Tournament.
Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.explanation: Per the official UPSC key the answer is (a) 1 and 2 only. (Note: VisionIAS marked this 'C / 1 only'; the official provisional key differs.)
questions.elimination_path: Mark each statement true/false first, then eliminate every option that contains a statement you can rule out.

questions.subject: SciTech
questions.text: Which one of the following pairs of semiconductor plants in India and their locations is NOT correctly matched?

options.A: CG Power and Industrial Solutions Pvt. Ltd. (with Renesas Electronics and STARS Microelectronics) : Gujarat
options.B: Tata Semiconductor Assembly and Test Pvt. Ltd. : Assam
options.C: HCL-Foxconn Joint Venture India Chip Ltd. : Madhya Pradesh

questions.explanation: The HCL-Foxconn joint venture is not in Madhya Pradesh — that pair is NOT correctly matched. The others are correct.
questions.elimination_path: Eliminate the clearly wrong options first, then choose between what remains.

questions.subject: Environment
questions.text:
Which of the following statements with regard to India's indigenous new high-resolution weather model, the "Bharat Forecast System," is/are correct?
1. Its objective is to generate forecasts at the Panchayats cluster level.
2. It was developed by IIT Delhi.
Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.explanation: The Bharat Forecast System aims for highly localised (Panchayat-cluster) forecasts (1). It was not developed by IIT Delhi (2 wrong) — it is an IITM/IMD effort.
questions.elimination_path: Mark each statement true/false first, then eliminate every option that contains a statement you can rule out.

questions.subject: CurrentAffairs
questions.text:
Consider the following statements with regard to the film Boong:
1. The film has recently won the British Academy of Film and Television Arts (BAFTA) Award in the Children's and Family Film category.
2. The film is directed by Lakshmipriya Devi.
3. This is the first Indian film to win a BAFTA award in the Children's and Family Film category.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Boong won the BAFTA Children's & Family award, was directed by Lakshmipriya Devi, and is the first Indian film to win in that category — all three correct.
questions.elimination_path: Mark each statement true/false first, then eliminate every option that contains a statement you can rule out.

questions.subject: SciTech
questions.text:
Which of the following statements regarding the features of blockchain technology are correct?
1. Records stored in the database may be made visible to relevant stakeholders without risk of alteration.
2. Copies of the entire database are stored on multiple computers on a network, syncing within seconds.
3. Consortium blockchain is a blend of public and private blockchains allowing selective data access.
4. Mathematical algorithms make it impossible to change or delete any data once recorded and accepted.
Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.explanation: Records are tamper-evident and visible to authorised stakeholders (1), distributed copies sync across nodes (2), and cryptography prevents alteration once accepted (4). Statement 3 better describes a hybrid, not consortium, blockchain (wrong).
questions.elimination_path: Mark each statement true/false first, then eliminate every option that contains a statement you can rule out.

questions.subject: Economy
questions.text: An e-commerce revenue model where the seller has control over pricing but doesn't keep products in stock and instead transfers customer orders and shipment details to a third-party supplier, who then ships the goods directly to the customer, is called:
questions.explanation: This is the dropshipping model — the seller controls pricing/orders but holds no inventory; a third-party supplier ships directly.
questions.elimination_path: Eliminate the clearly wrong options first, then choose between what remains.

questions.subject: Economy
questions.text: Which one of the following correctly represents the three key sub-indices of the Financial Inclusion Index (FI-Index) of the Reserve Bank of India (RBI)?

options.A: Credit access, Insurance depth, and Pension coverage
options.B: Banking access, GDP contribution, and Financial literacy

questions.explanation: The RBI FI-Index rests on three parameters — Access, Usage and Quality.
questions.elimination_path: Eliminate the clearly wrong options first, then choose between what remains.

questions.subject: Economy
questions.text: Which one of the following best describes the key objective of India's "Open Network for Digital Commerce" (ONDC) initiative?

options.A: To allow government control over all digital commerce transactions
options.C: To break the dominance of large e-commerce platforms by enabling interoperability across networks
options.D: To mandate UPI-based payments for all online transactions

questions.explanation: ONDC aims to make digital commerce open and interoperable, breaking the dominance of large closed platforms.
questions.elimination_path: Eliminate the clearly wrong options first, then choose between what remains.

questions.subject: Economy
questions.text: Which one of the following statements about Unified Payments Interface (UPI) and Central Bank Digital Currency (Digital Rupee) is NOT correct?

options.A: UPI is a real-time payment system but Digital Rupee is akin to sovereign paper currency.
options.B: In case of UPI, settlement for end users happens instantly as the money gets immediately debited or credited but in the case of Digital Rupee, there is no settlement as the wallet balance gets transferred to another wallet.
options.C: UPI transactions are recorded by banks and reflected in bank statements but in case of Digital Rupee, no data is captured in bank statements as transactions are from one wallet to another.
options.D: In both the cases, UPI and Digital Rupee, the liability lies with the users and their respective banks.

questions.explanation: Digital Rupee is central-bank money, so its liability lies with the RBI — not with commercial banks. Hence statement (d) is NOT correct.
questions.elimination_path: Eliminate the clearly wrong options first, then choose between what remains.

questions.subject: Economy
questions.text:
Which of the following statements about Real-World Assets (RWA) Tokenization are correct?
1. Tokenization is the process of turning real world assets into digital tokens using blockchain technology.
2. Tokenization of real world assets offers 24×7 access, promoting financial inclusion.
3. Tokenization of real world assets will allow the access to high growth investment opportunities for individuals in India.
Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.explanation: RWA tokenisation digitises real assets on a blockchain, enabling 24×7 access, wider participation and access to high-growth opportunities — all three correct.
questions.elimination_path: Mark each statement true/false first, then eliminate every option that contains a statement you can rule out.

questions.subject: Economy
questions.text: A bond whose proceeds are used only to finance or refinance a combination of both environmental and social projects is called:
questions.explanation: A sustainability bond finances a mix of environmental and social projects; green bonds are environment-only and social bonds are social-only.
questions.elimination_path: Eliminate the clearly wrong options first, then choose between what remains.

questions.subject: Economy
questions.text:
Which of the following statements about M1xchange's role in Micro, Small & Medium Enterprises (MSMEs) financing is/are correct?
1. M1xchange provides collateral based loans to MSMEs.
2. M1xchange facilitates discounting of invoices and Bills of Exchange for MSMEs.
3. M1xchange functions as a credit rating agency for MSMEs.
Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.explanation: M1xchange is a TReDS platform facilitating invoice/bill discounting (2). It does not give collateral-based loans or act as a rating agency (1, 3 wrong).
questions.elimination_path: Mark each statement true/false first, then eliminate every option that contains a statement you can rule out.

questions.subject: Economy
questions.text: Which one of the following best describes the "Crowding Out Effect" in the context of fiscal policy?

options.A: A situation where private investment increases due to increased Government spending
options.B: A situation where Government borrowing leads to higher interest rates, which reduces private investment
options.C: A situation where an increase in taxes leads to increased private sector investment
options.D: A situation where Government spending has no impact on aggregate demand

questions.explanation: Crowding out: heavy government borrowing pushes up interest rates, reducing private investment.
questions.elimination_path: Eliminate the clearly wrong options first, then choose between what remains.

questions.subject: Economy
questions.text:
Which of the following statements about Rare Earth Elements (REEs) and Critical Minerals is/are correct?
1. Modern technological innovations including Artificial Intelligence, robotics and space exploration extensively utilise Rare Earth Elements (REEs).
2. China has the highest share in mining of REEs followed by India.
3. The Government of India launched the National Critical Mineral Mission (NCMM) in 2025 to establish a robust framework for self-reliance in the critical mineral sector.
4. Rare Earth Elements are a set of 13 metallic elements.
Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.explanation: REEs are vital for AI/robotics/space (1) and India launched NCMM in 2025 (3). China is NOT followed by India in mining share (2 wrong), and REEs are 17 elements, not 13 (4 wrong).
questions.elimination_path: Mark each statement true/false first, then eliminate every option that contains a statement you can rule out.

questions.subject: Economy
questions.text:
Which of the following statements about insurance in aviation sector is/are correct?
1. "Aviation Hull Insurance" covers the physical aircraft, including the body, engine, and on-board equipment.
2. Under the Montreal Convention, adopted in 1999 by over 130 countries, including India, airlines are strictly liable to pay compensation to the family/nominee of every deceased passenger without requiring the family to prove fault.
Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.explanation: Aviation Hull Insurance covers the physical aircraft (1), and the Montreal Convention imposes strict no-fault liability for passenger death/injury (2). Both correct.
questions.elimination_path: Mark each statement true/false first, then eliminate every option that contains a statement you can rule out.

questions.subject: Economy
questions.text:
Which of the following statements about Crowdfunding is/are correct?
1. Crowdfunding is solicitation of funds (small amount) from multiple investors through a web-based platform or social networking site for a specific project.
2. Small and Medium Enterprises (SMEs) are able to raise funds at lower cost of capital without undergoing rigorous procedures.
Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.explanation: Crowdfunding raises small amounts from many people via online platforms (1) and can help SMEs raise funds at lower cost with fewer procedures (2). Both correct.
questions.elimination_path: Mark each statement true/false first, then eliminate every option that contains a statement you can rule out.

questions.subject: Economy
questions.text:
With reference to different Committees in India, consider the following details:
1. R.N. Malhotra Committee — Comprehensive reforms of Insurance sector — IRDAI
2. L.C. Gupta Committee — Roadmap for introduction of derivatives trading — SEBI
3. Urjit R. Patel Committee — Roadmap for reforming bank lending to the Housing sector — RBI
4. Y.H. Malegam Committee — Roadmap for reforms in Microfinance sector — RBI
In how many of the above rows are all the details correctly matched?
questions.explanation: Rows 2 (L.C. Gupta–derivatives–SEBI) and 4 (Malegam–microfinance–RBI) are correctly matched. Malhotra was not under IRDAI, and Urjit Patel dealt with the monetary-policy framework, not housing. So 2 and 4.
questions.elimination_path: Mark each statement true/false first, then eliminate every option that contains a statement you can rule out.

questions.subject: Economy
questions.text:
Consider the following statements about the Non-Banking Financial Companies (NBFCs) in India:
1. NBFCs cannot accept demand deposits.
2. All the NBFCs operating in India have to be registered with the RBI.
3. NBFCs form part of the payment and settlement system and can issue cheque drawn on itself.
4. Deposit insurance facility of DICGC is not available to the depositors of deposit taking NBFCs.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: NBFCs cannot accept demand deposits (1) and DICGC insurance is unavailable to NBFC depositors (4). Not all NBFC-type entities register with the RBI (2 wrong), and NBFCs are not part of the payment & settlement system (3 wrong).
questions.elimination_path: Mark each statement true/false first, then eliminate every option that contains a statement you can rule out.

questions.subject: Economy
questions.text:
Consider the following statements about Multidimensional Poverty Index (MPI):
1. MPI is calculated using Alkire-Foster methodology.
2. MPI calculated by NITI Aayog has a total of twelve indicators.
3. Maternal Health and Bank Account are common indicators in the MPI of NITI Aayog and MPI of United Nations Development Programme (UNDP).
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: MPI uses the Alkire-Foster method (1) and NITI Aayog's National MPI has 12 indicators (2). Maternal Health and Bank Account are not common to both the NITI and UNDP indices (3 wrong).
questions.elimination_path: Mark each statement true/false first, then eliminate every option that contains a statement you can rule out.

questions.subject: SciTech
questions.text:
Which of the following statements with regard to genetic medicine is/are correct?
1. Genetic medicines correct/compensate for the faulty genes responsible for disease.
2. Engineered viruses and lipid nanoparticles are used as carriers of the genetic medicine.
3. Genetic medicines alter the entire DNA sequence.
Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.explanation: Genetic medicines correct/compensate faulty genes (1) and use viral vectors and lipid nanoparticles as carriers (2). They do not alter the entire DNA sequence (3 wrong).
questions.elimination_path: Mark each statement true/false first, then eliminate every option that contains a statement you can rule out.

questions.subject: SciTech
questions.text:
Which of the following statements with regard to Large Language Models (LLMs) used in machine learning is/are correct?
1. LLMs assign probabilities to the next possible words and then pick the one with the highest probability.
2. LLMs process data through mathematical optimization to minimise prediction errors.
3. LLMs produce unbiased outputs.
Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.explanation: LLMs assign next-token probabilities (1) and train via optimisation to cut prediction error (2). They can reproduce training-data bias, so they are not unbiased (3 wrong).
questions.elimination_path: Mark each statement true/false first, then eliminate every option that contains a statement you can rule out.

questions.subject: SciTech
questions.text:
Which of the following statements with regard to stealth technology is/are correct?
1. Stealth objects have a very small radar cross-section and are coated with Radar Absorbing Material.
2. Stealth objects can be detected using specific frequencies.
3. Stealth objects are coated with metamaterials to increase the scattering of electromagnetic radiation.
Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.explanation: Stealth reduces radar cross-section with absorbing materials (1) and can still be detected at certain frequencies (2). Metamaterials are used to REDUCE/redirect, not increase, scattering (3 wrong).
questions.elimination_path: Mark each statement true/false first, then eliminate every option that contains a statement you can rule out.

questions.subject: SciTech
questions.text:
Which of the following statements with regard to Black Boxes used in modern aircrafts is/are correct?
1. They carry a beacon emitting red light pulses to facilitate underwater detection.
2. They record both the cockpit voice and flight data.
3. Their memory units are made using either stainless steel or titanium.
Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.explanation: Black boxes record cockpit voice and flight data (2) and use hardened stainless-steel/titanium memory units (3). Their underwater locator beacon emits acoustic pings, not red light (1 wrong).
questions.elimination_path: Mark each statement true/false first, then eliminate every option that contains a statement you can rule out.

questions.subject: Environment
questions.text:
Which of the following statements with regard to Green Hydrogen is/are correct?
1. It is decarbonized hydrogen obtained from natural gas reforming combined with carbon capture and storage (CCS).
2. It is produced using electrolysis of water with electricity generated by renewable energy.
3. National Green Hydrogen Mission of India aims for abatement of nearly 50 MMT of annual greenhouse gas emissions by 2030.
Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.explanation: Green hydrogen is made by renewable-powered electrolysis (2) and the Mission targets ~50 MMT abatement by 2030 (3). Natural-gas reforming with CCS is BLUE hydrogen (1 wrong).
questions.elimination_path: Mark each statement true/false first, then eliminate every option that contains a statement you can rule out.

questions.subject: SciTech
questions.text:
Consider the following statements with regard to involvement of private entities in India's space programme:
1. The Indian National Space Promotion and Authorisation Centre (IN-SPACe) is an autonomous agency formed to facilitate participation of private entities.
2. Agnikul Cosmos launched the world's first flight using 3D-printed rocket engine.
3. Skyroot Aerospace has developed liquid fuel for GSLV.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: IN-SPACe authorises private participation (1) and Agnikul flew the first 3D-printed-engine rocket (2). Skyroot has not developed liquid fuel for GSLV (3 wrong).
questions.elimination_path: Mark each statement true/false first, then eliminate every option that contains a statement you can rule out.

questions.subject: SciTech
questions.text:
Which of the following statements with regard to drone swarms is/are correct?
1. They use Terahertz band of frequency to communicate with the command centre.
2. Individual drones in the swarm can communicate with other drones in the swarm.
3. GPS Spoofing is a commonly used technique to counter drone swarm attack.
Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.explanation: Swarm drones communicate with one another (2) and GPS spoofing is a counter-drone technique (3). The Terahertz-band command claim is not generally correct (1 wrong).
questions.elimination_path: Mark each statement true/false first, then eliminate every option that contains a statement you can rule out.

questions.subject: SciTech
questions.text:
Which of the following statements with regard to GenomeIndia Project is/are correct?
1. It is a part of the Human Genome Project.
2. The project is funded by the Department of Biotechnology (DBT), Government of India.
3. Its primary aim is to build a catalogue of genetic diversity of the Indian population.
Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.explanation: GenomeIndia is DBT-funded (2) and aims to catalogue Indian genetic diversity (3). It is not a part of the Human Genome Project (1 wrong).
questions.elimination_path: Mark each statement true/false first, then eliminate every option that contains a statement you can rule out.

questions.subject: SciTech
questions.text:
Which of the following statements with regard to the National Quantum Mission (NQM) is/are correct?
1. It aims at developing intermediate-scale quantum computers with 50–1000 physical qubits.
2. Its implementation includes setting up of four Thematic Hubs (T-Hubs) in academic and national R&D institutes across India.
Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.explanation: NQM targets intermediate-scale quantum computers of 50–1000 qubits (1) and sets up four Thematic Hubs (2). Both correct.
questions.elimination_path: Mark each statement true/false first, then eliminate every option that contains a statement you can rule out.

questions.subject: SciTech
questions.text:
Which of the following statements with regard to India's Deep Ocean Mission is/are correct?
1. It was launched by the Ministry of Ports, Shipping and Waterways, Government of India.
2. Matsya-6000 has been designed to carry 3 people for deep sea exploration.
3. Samudrayaan is a project under this mission.
Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.explanation: Matsya-6000 carries 3 people (2) and Samudrayaan is part of the mission (3). It is run by the Ministry of Earth Sciences, not Ports/Shipping (1 wrong).
questions.elimination_path: Mark each statement true/false first, then eliminate every option that contains a statement you can rule out.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.text:
Mr. X, a senior officer overseeing a critical vaccination programme during a pandemic, found a private service provider compromising on quality to make profits. Despite pressure due to vested interests, he reported the issue to the appropriate vigilance authority and halted the contract to ensure citizen welfare.
Which one among the following principles of public administration was most strongly demonstrated by Mr. X's actions?
questions.explanation: By reporting wrongdoing, halting a compromised contract and prioritising citizen welfare despite pressure, Mr. X demonstrated accountability.
questions.elimination_path: Eliminate the clearly wrong options first, then choose between what remains.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.text:
A Government decision to allocate land for a waste-management facility near a tribal hamlet has sparked protests (the land is claimed as sacred), while urban residents and industries support it. As the official tasked with resolving this through mediation, consider:
1. A successful conflict-resolution process must begin with acknowledging the cultural concerns of the protesting tribal community before discussing technical alternatives.
2. The Government should move ahead with the project without delay to address urban health concerns, which outweigh the sentiments of a small group.
3. Creating a multi-stakeholder dialogue platform — including tribal leaders, environmental experts and municipal representatives — to build mutual understanding and de-escalate tensions.
4. Conducting an independent Environmental and Social Impact Assessment (ESIA) and sharing findings transparently with both sides.
Which of the statements given above would contribute to the resolution process?
questions.explanation: Acknowledging cultural concerns (1), a multi-stakeholder dialogue (3) and a transparent independent ESIA (4) build resolution. Forcing the project through unilaterally (2) would worsen the conflict.
questions.elimination_path: Mark each statement true/false first, then eliminate every option that contains a statement you can rule out.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.text:
Ms. X, approving a contract for a community park, receives confidential information that a shortlisted contractor has a history of poor workmanship and unproven corruption allegations elsewhere. Her HoD advises non-disclosure to avoid delay and reputational damage; she believes withholding it harms transparency.
What amongst the following should Ms. X do now?
1. Immediately disclose the information to the project committee and the public.
2. Recommend removing the contractor from the shortlist to protect the project's integrity.
3. Propose a "limited disclosure" to an oversight committee, while keeping the information confidential from the public for the time being.
Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.explanation: As the allegations are unproven, public disclosure or removal would be unfair. The balanced course is limited disclosure to an oversight committee while preserving confidentiality for now (3).
questions.elimination_path: Mark each statement true/false first, then eliminate every option that contains a statement you can rule out.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.text:
'X', addressing a seminar on the term 'law' under Article 13, Part III, said it includes ordinances, orders, rules and regulations. 'Y' pointed out that 'law' in Article 13 also includes custom or usage having the force of law in India, to which 'X' was not convinced.
Based on the above, select the correct conclusion:

options.A: 'X' is correct in the interpretation of law, including the view on non-inclusion of custom.
options.B: The view of 'Y' that 'law' included custom is not correct.
options.C: The views of both 'X' and 'Y' are correct.
options.D: The view of only 'Y' is correct.

questions.explanation: Under Article 13, 'law' includes ordinances, orders, rules, regulations, notifications, customs and usages having the force of law. Since 'X' rejected custom but 'Y' included it, only 'Y' is correct.
questions.elimination_path: Eliminate the clearly wrong options first, then choose between what remains.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.text:
Consider the following statements with reference to the Constitution of India:
1. There is no Article in the Constitution of India that specifies that the Constitution of India will be officially called the "Constitution of India".
2. There is no Article in the Constitution of India that specifies that the Indian Independence Act, 1947 and the Government of India Act, 1935 stand repealed.
3. There is no Article in the Constitution of India that mentions 26th January, 1950 as the date of the commencement of the Constitution of India.
Which one of the following conclusions based on the above statements is correct?

options.C: There are two correct statements that include statement 3.
options.D: There is only one correct statement.

questions.explanation: All three statements are FALSE — the Constitution does provide for its short title, the repeal of the 1947/1935 Acts (Article 395) and the commencement date (Article 394). Hence "there is no correct statement".
questions.elimination_path: Mark each statement true/false first, then eliminate every option that contains a statement you can rule out.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.text:
Which of the following statements with regard to the persons with disabilities in India is/are correct?
1. The Rights of Persons with Disabilities Act, an Act passed by the Parliament of India in 2018, mandates reservation in education and employment and places a legal duty on Governments to ensure accessibility and non-discrimination.
2. The Sugamya Bharat Abhiyan focuses on achieving universal accessibility for Persons with Disabilities across three key domains — built infrastructure, transport systems and information and communication technology.
3. The National Divyangjan Finance and Development Corporation (NDFDC) is a public sector organisation set up by the Ministry of Corporate Affairs as a not-for-profit company.
Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.explanation: Sugamya Bharat Abhiyan covers built infrastructure, transport and ICT (2 correct). The RPwD Act was passed in 2016, not 2018 (1 wrong); NDFDC is not under the Ministry of Corporate Affairs (3 wrong).
questions.elimination_path: Mark each statement true/false first, then eliminate every option that contains a statement you can rule out.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.text:
Consider the following statements about the provisions pertaining to the Scheduled Castes and the Scheduled Tribes in India:
1. Provisions regarding the administration of the Tribal Areas in the States of Assam, Meghalaya, Tripura and Mizoram are given in the Fifth Schedule of the Constitution of India.
2. Some tribes of India are entitled to exemption from paying Income Tax on certain incomes.
3. The Constitution of India provides for reservation of seats in Panchayats for women belonging to the Scheduled Castes and the Scheduled Tribes.
Which one of the following conclusions based on the above statements is correct?

options.A: There are two correct statements, that include statement 2.
options.B: There are two correct statements, that are statements 1 and 3.
options.C: There is only one correct statement.

questions.explanation: Statement 1 is wrong — those tribal areas are under the SIXTH Schedule, not the Fifth. Statements 2 (income-tax exemption) and 3 (Panchayat reservation for SC/ST women) are correct. So two correct statements, including statement 2.
questions.elimination_path: Mark each statement true/false first, then eliminate every option that contains a statement you can rule out.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.text:
Consider the following statements in respect of questions asked by the Members in the Parliament of India:
1. Unstarred questions are those to which a Member desires an oral answer in the House.
2. Starred questions are those to which a Member desires a written answer.
3. No supplementary question can be asked on an unstarred question.
Which one of the following conclusions based on the above statements is correct?

options.A: All the three statements are correct.
options.B: There are two correct statements, that include statement 2.
options.C: There is only one correct statement.

questions.explanation: Statements 1 and 2 are swapped — starred = oral (with supplementaries), unstarred = written. Only statement 3 (no supplementary on unstarred) is correct.
questions.elimination_path: Mark each statement true/false first, then eliminate every option that contains a statement you can rule out.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.text:
Consider the following statements about the Committee on the Welfare of Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes of the Parliament of India:
1. Although members are elected from both Houses, the Chairperson of this Committee is appointed by the Chairman of the Rajya Sabha.
2. Twenty members are elected by the Rajya Sabha and ten members by the Lok Sabha.
3. No Minister, except for the Union Minister of Social Justice and Empowerment, is eligible to be a member of this Committee.
4. Members are elected for a fixed term of two years from the date they enter their office.
Which one of the following conclusions based on the above statements is correct?

options.B: There is only one correct statement, that is statement 2.
options.C: There are two correct statements, that include statement 1.

questions.explanation: The Chairperson is appointed by the Speaker (not the Rajya Sabha Chairman); the composition is 20 Lok Sabha + 10 Rajya Sabha (reverse of statement 2); no Minister can be a member; and the term is one year. All four statements are wrong — no correct statement.
questions.elimination_path: Mark each statement true/false first, then eliminate every option that contains a statement you can rule out.

questions.subject: SciTech
questions.text:
Consider the following statements about Mission Sudarshan Chakra of India:
1. It aims to enhance India's air defence, ballistic missile defence and aerial offensive capabilities.
2. This Mission is being designed to enhance rapid, precise, and powerful defence responses, reinforcing India's strategic autonomy.
3. One of the aims of this Mission is to cover all public places of India by an expanded nationwide security shield by 2035.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Mission Sudarshan Chakra aims to strengthen air defence, BMD and offensive capability (1), enable rapid precise responses reinforcing strategic autonomy (2), and extend a nationwide security shield by 2035 (3). All correct.
questions.elimination_path: Mark each statement true/false first, then eliminate every option that contains a statement you can rule out.

questions.subject: CurrentAffairs
questions.text:
Consider the following statements about river bridges connecting India with neighbouring countries:
1. "Maitri Setu", built over Feni river, connects Ramgarh in India with Sabroom in Bangladesh.
2. Jhulaghat suspension bridge connects India with Myanmar.
3. Mechi bridge and its approaches connect Panitanki Bypass in India with Kakarvitta in Nepal.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Maitri Setu connects Sabroom (India) with Ramgarh (Bangladesh) — statement 1 reverses the locations. Jhulaghat connects India–Nepal, not Myanmar (2 wrong). Only Mechi bridge (3) is correct.
questions.elimination_path: Mark each statement true/false first, then eliminate every option that contains a statement you can rule out.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.text:
Which of the following statements about a Zero First Information Report (Zero FIR) under the Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita (BNSS), 2023 is/are correct?
1. A Zero FIR can be lodged at a police station, even though the place of commission of a cognizable/non-cognizable offence is outside the territorial jurisdiction of that police station.
2. The Officer-in-Charge of the police station where a Zero FIR has been lodged may, with the permission of the competent authority, initiate a preliminary enquiry.
3. Under Zero FIR, it is obligatory for the informant to furnish information electronically.
Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.explanation: A Zero FIR pertains to cognizable offences, so statement 1 (which adds "non-cognizable") is wrong; electronic furnishing is not obligatory (3 wrong). Only the preliminary-enquiry provision (2) is correct.
questions.elimination_path: Mark each statement true/false first, then eliminate every option that contains a statement you can rule out.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.text:
With reference to the organisations under the Government of India, consider the following details:
1. Central Economic Intelligence Bureau (CEIB) — To coordinate between various law enforcement agencies — Ministry of Home Affairs
2. Serious Fraud Investigation Office (SFIO) — To investigate complex corporate frauds — Ministry of Finance
3. Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI) — To preserve values in public life and ensure the health of the national economy — Ministry of Personnel, Public Grievances and Pension
In how many of the above rows are the given details correctly matched?
questions.explanation: Only one row is fully correct (CBI under DoPT). CEIB is under the Ministry of Finance (not Home), and SFIO is under the Ministry of Corporate Affairs (not Finance).
questions.elimination_path: Mark each statement true/false first, then eliminate every option that contains a statement you can rule out.

questions.subject: CurrentAffairs
questions.text:
Which of the following international conventions have NOT been ratified by India?
1. Employment Policy Convention
2. Abolition of Forced Labour Convention
3. International Convention on the Protection of the Rights of All Migrant Workers and Members of Their Families
4. Geneva Convention Relative to the Protection of Civilian Persons in Time of War
5. Convention on Reduction of Statelessness
Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.explanation: This question was OFFICIALLY DROPPED by UPSC — no listed option matches the correct set (the conventions not ratified by India are 3 and 5 only). All candidates were awarded marks. (Answer shown is a placeholder.)
questions.elimination_path: Mark each statement true/false first, then eliminate every option that contains a statement you can rule out.

questions.subject: SciTech
questions.text:
Consider the following statements with respect to the AI Impact Summit, 2026 held in New Delhi:
1. The Summit's intellectual framework was based on three foundational Sutras: People, Planning, and Progress.
2. The Preamble of the Summit acknowledges the Charter for Democratic Diffusion of AI as a binding framework.
3. The New Delhi Declaration on AI Impact was structured around seven Access Chakras (Pillars), which included Access for Social Empowerment, AI for Science, and Secure and Trusted AI.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: The Sutras were People, Planet and Prosperity (not "Planning and Progress") — 1 wrong. The Charter was not binding — 2 wrong. Only the seven Access Chakras framing (3) is correct.
questions.elimination_path: Mark each statement true/false first, then eliminate every option that contains a statement you can rule out.

questions.subject: CurrentAffairs
questions.text:
Which of the following connectivity projects is/are a part of cooperation between India and the ASEAN member countries?
1. Kaladan Multi-Modal Transit Transport Project
2. IMT Trilateral Highway
3. Agartala-Akhaura Rail Line
Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.explanation: Kaladan (1) and IMT Trilateral Highway (2) run through Myanmar (ASEAN). Agartala-Akhaura (3) is an India–Bangladesh project, not ASEAN.
questions.elimination_path: Mark each statement true/false first, then eliminate every option that contains a statement you can rule out.

questions.subject: CurrentAffairs
questions.text:
Match List I with List II and select the answer using the code given below the Lists:
List I (Project Supported by India): A. Mangdechhu Hydroelectric Project  B. Restoration of Stor Palace  C. District Hospital at Dickoya  D. Institute of Security and Law Enforcement Studies
List II (Country): 1. Maldives  2. Afghanistan  3. Bhutan  4. Sri Lanka
questions.explanation: Mangdechhu — Bhutan (3), Stor Palace — Afghanistan (2), District Hospital Dickoya — Sri Lanka (4), Institute of Security & Law Enforcement Studies — Maldives (1).
questions.elimination_path: Mark each statement true/false first, then eliminate every option that contains a statement you can rule out.

questions.subject: SciTech
questions.text:
Which of the following items of defence hardware is/are manufactured in India?
1. Su-30 MKI Fighter Jets
2. T-90 MK-III Tanks
3. Akula Class Submarine
Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.explanation: Su-30 MKI jets and T-90 tanks are licence-manufactured/assembled in India (1, 2). Akula-class submarines are leased from Russia, not made in India (3 wrong).
questions.elimination_path: Mark each statement true/false first, then eliminate every option that contains a statement you can rule out.

questions.subject: CurrentAffairs
questions.text:
Consider the following statements about platforms for multilateral co-operation:
1. The "Colombo Process" is a regional consultative process in which member states take binding decisions by consensus.
2. The "Abu Dhabi Dialogue" is a voluntary non-binding consultative process among Asian countries of labour origin and destination to facilitate regional cooperation on contractual labour mobility.
3. The "Global Forum for Migration and Development", created upon the proposal of a former UN Secretary General, is a voluntary forum whose decisions are non-binding in nature.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: The Abu Dhabi Dialogue (2) and the GFMD (3) are voluntary and non-binding. The Colombo Process is also consultative and non-binding, so statement 1 ("binding decisions") is wrong.
questions.elimination_path: Mark each statement true/false first, then eliminate every option that contains a statement you can rule out.

questions.subject: CurrentAffairs
questions.text:
Consider the following UN organisations/agencies:
1. World Food Programme
2. United Nations Children's Fund
3. United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees
4. International Labour Organisation
How many of the above has/have been awarded the Nobel Prize twice?
questions.explanation: Only UNHCR has won the Nobel Peace Prize twice. WFP, UNICEF and ILO have each won it once.
questions.elimination_path: Mark each statement true/false first, then eliminate every option that contains a statement you can rule out.

questions.subject: CurrentAffairs
questions.text:
Match List I with List II and select the answer using the code given below the Lists:
List I (UN Peacekeeping Operation): A. UNMIL  B. MINURCAT  C. MINUSTAH  D. UNMISET
List II (Period of Operation): 1. 2007–2010  2. 2002–2005  3. 2003–2018  4. 2004–2017
questions.explanation: UNMIL (Liberia) 2003–2018, MINURCAT (Chad/CAR) 2007–2010, MINUSTAH (Haiti) 2004–2017, UNMISET (Timor-Leste) 2002–2005.
questions.elimination_path: Mark each statement true/false first, then eliminate every option that contains a statement you can rule out.

questions.subject: CurrentAffairs
questions.text:
Match List I with List II and select the answer using the code given below the Lists:

questions.subject: CurrentAffairs
questions.text: Which one of the following pairs is NOT correctly matched? (Indian Army Corps : Headquarters)
questions.explanation: 33 Corps is headquartered at Sukna (near Siliguri), not Srinagar — so that pair is NOT correctly matched.
questions.elimination_path: Eliminate the clearly wrong options first, then choose between what remains.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.text:
Which of the following statements with respect to the Revamped Rashtriya Gram Swaraj Abhiyan (RGSA) is/are correct?
1. The period of its implementation is 1st April, 2021 to 31st March, 2026.
2. The key objective of the Revamped RGSA is to develop the governance capabilities of the Panchayati Raj Institutions to deliver on the Sustainable Development Goals.
3. The share of the Central funding for the Revamped RGSA is 100% for all States and Union Territories.
Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.explanation: RGSA aims to build PRI governance capacity to deliver the SDGs (2 correct). The implementation period in statement 1 is wrong, and central funding is not 100% for all States/UTs (3 wrong).
questions.elimination_path: Mark each statement true/false first, then eliminate every option that contains a statement you can rule out.

questions.subject: CurrentAffairs
questions.text:
Which of the following countries are members of the European Union?
1. Belarus
2. Poland
3. Germany
4. Switzerland
Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.explanation: Poland and Germany are EU members (2, 3). Belarus and Switzerland are not.
questions.elimination_path: Mark each statement true/false first, then eliminate every option that contains a statement you can rule out.
